extraction.one_line_summary: JASPER Philipsen won the ceremonial Champs-Élysées sprint on Stage 21 after VINGEGAARD, POGAČAR, VAN AERT, and GESCHKE performed a publicity attack at the neutral start.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD orchestrated fake breakaway with jersey wearers after km zero, prioritizing media spectacle over racing—risky precedent undermining competitive integrity.
VAN AERT calling GESCHKE into photo-op break despite not owning polka dots reveals Jumbo-Visma's comfort controlling narrative even off-script.
Stage 21 sprint finish commentary completely omitted, suggesting lead-out positioning data unavailable—future analysis requires different video sources.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: orchestrated ceremonial attack and controlled publicity narrative, demonstrating team's psychological dominance extended beyond pure racing
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: participated in staged attack recreation of competitive aggression, possibly normalizing relationship after three-week battle
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: secured green jersey emphatically, strong enough to dictate photo opportunities and control stage narrative
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: neutral: called into jersey photo despite finishing second in KOM, revealing breakaway specialists need one fewer stage win to claim classification
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: third at 36 with best-ever numbers suggests late-career peak possible with reduced pressure, contrasts aging curve assumptions
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: carried ceremonially after early crashes ended GC bid, exposes Jumbo-Visma's reliance on dual leadership to absorb bad luck
